name: TPC Michigan
about: TPC Michigan is a championship golf destination designed by Jack Nicklaus, featuring an 18-hole course and a 3-tiered grass practice facility. The club emphasizes a lifestyle investment with a focus on both competitive play and professional networking. Inside the clubhouse, the Golden Bear Lounge provides year-round simulated golf games, dining, and social spaces, fostering an environment for relationship-building and community engagement.
why_golfers_like_it: Designed by Jack Nicklaus offering a championship-level challenge
Access to the Invited Network with hundreds of country clubs nationwide
Year-round Golden Bear Lounge with simulators and dining options
Reduced monthly dues promotion through March 2026 for new members

food_and_drink.notes: Full dining room and bar with executive chef-led restaurant, 50% off a la carte dining for members
Craft drinks available at the Golden Bear Lounge and bar

faq.question: Do I need to bring my own clubs?
faq.answer: The club provides access to its championship golf course and practice facilities. Members typically use their own equipment, but rentals may be available upon inquiry.

faq.question: Can I bring my own food and drinks?
faq.answer: Outside food and drinks are not mentioned, but the club offers an executive chef-led restaurant and bar with craft drinks. Members receive 50% off a la carte dining.

faq.question: How long does it take to play 18 holes?
faq.answer: While specific timing is not provided, the 18-hole Jack Nicklaus championship course is described as challenging and competitive, with typical play times varying based on pace.

faq.question: Is the venue kid-friendly?
faq.answer: Yes, the membership includes family access and may cover spouse and dependents under 25, making it suitable for families. The club emphasizes community and social events.
